items.summary: Citius TransNet Investment Trust is a transport-focused infrastructure investment trust set up to acquire, manage and invest in road and other transport assets across India.

items.title: YES Bank Q4 preview: NII to grow up to 12%; PAT estimates diverge. 8 things to watch
items.summary: YES Bank is poised for a steady Q4FY26 performance, with brokerages forecasting healthy earnings growth driven by stable margins and resilient loan expansion. Analysts anticipate a profit after tax between Rs 765 crore and Rs 1,066 crore, reflecting significant year-on-year growth.

items.summary: From Cuba to the Persian Gulf, Trump is expected to face fresh challenges across a new arc of tanker diplomacy.

items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Price LIVE: Equity benchmarks continued to trade in positive territory on Friday’s afternoon session, supported by optimism around easing geopolitical tensions and fresh foreign fund inflows.

items.title: Rupee rises 29 paise to settle at 92.85 against US dollar
items.summary: The unit touched the day's low of 92.98 before ending the session at 92.85 (provisional) against the American currency, up 29 paise from the previous closing level

items.title: Andhra Government clears Rs 2,500 crore NSPSL cathode plant in Chittoor
items.summary: Andhra Pradesh is set to boost its electronics and battery sector. The state government has approved a significant Rs 2,550 crore investment by NSPSL Speciality Chemicals. This will establish a cathode material manufacturing facility in Chittoor district. This project is crucial for localizing key battery components. It will support the growth of electric vehicles and consumer electronics.

items.title: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
items.summary: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

items.title: Walmart's Flipkart plans foray into India's ticketing market as live events boom, sources say
items.summary: Walmart-owned Flipkart is set to enter India's burgeoning movie and concert ticket market, aiming for a May launch. This move into the fast-growing entertainment sector will see Flipkart compete with established players like BookMyShow and Zomato's District. The company is also piloting food delivery, signaling expansion into competitive, low-margin businesses.

items.title: India’s current account deficit may rise to 2% of GDP in FY27 if oil stays at $82–87: CRISIL
items.summary: Rising oil prices could push India's current account deficit to a concerning two percent of GDP, as highlighted by a Crisil report. This prediction hinges on the volatile global market conditions we’re currently facing. While US tariff reductions may bolster exports to some extent, the ongoing unrest in West Asia is another pivotal aspect that could affect the situation.

items.title: Not seeking to suppress legitimate product reviews, consumer feedback: Motorola on defamation case
items.summary: Motorola India has apologized to content creators inadvertently affected by its legal action against malicious reviews. The company initiated a civil defamation suit and temporary injunction against posts containing false claims of device explosions, aiming to prevent public alarm and protect its reputation. Motorola clarified it does not intend to suppress legitimate reviews and is committed to resolving product issues.

items.title: Vedanta was never declared highest bidder for Jaiprakash Associates: RP to NCLAT
items.summary: Jaiprakash Associates insolvency proceedings see a dispute over Vedanta's claim as highest bidder. The resolution professional told the NCLAT that no formal declaration was made. An email only showed the highest financial value discovered. Vedanta's petition is argued to lack legal and factual basis.

items.title: Bajaj Consumer Care shares soar 16% after Q4 net profit doubles to Rs 63 crore
items.summary: Bajaj Consumer Care shares surged significantly on Friday. The company reported a substantial increase in its net profit, more than doubling compared to the previous year. Revenue from operations also saw a healthy jump. This strong financial performance drove the rally in its stock price. The company also presented robust full-year results, indicating positive growth trends.

items.title: India seen as stable global capital hub with $4.4 trillion market cap: SEBI chief
items.summary: India is increasingly recognized as a stable global capital hub with its market capitalization reaching USD 4.4 trillion and a total of USD 154 billion raised through equity and debt markets in FY26, according to Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey.

items.title: Why stock market rose today? Sensex jumps 500 points, Nifty settles above 24,350. 5 key factors explained
items.summary: Indian stock markets saw significant gains on Friday. The Sensex and Nifty closed the week over 2% higher. This recovery follows a March selloff. Bulls are gaining momentum amid hopes for a quicker end to the Iran-US conflict. The market capitalization of BSE-listed companies increased substantially, adding over Rs 53 lakh crore this week.

items.title: NDR InvIT acquires Grade-A warehousing assets in Kochi, Coimbatore for Rs 260 crore
items.summary: NDR InvIT Trust has acquired two warehousing assets in Kochi and Coimbatore for approximately ₹260 crore. This expansion adds about 0.79 million sq ft of leasable area. The company's total operating area now stands at 22.96 million sq ft. This move strengthens NDR InvIT's presence in high-growth South Indian markets. The acquisition includes fully occupied assets with stable lease agreements.

items.title: Windlas Biotech announces Rs 47 crore buyback at Rs 1,000/share; sets April 24 as record date
items.summary: Windlas Biotech approved a Rs 47 crore share buyback at Rs 1,000 per share via the tender route, with April 24 set as the record date. The move covers 2.23% equity and excludes promoters. Shares rose 2% following the announcement, reflecting positive investor sentiment toward the capital return plan.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on April 17: Angel One, Triveni Turbine, Zen Tech, Emami, OLA, Suzlon Energy among top gainers
items.summary: Indian stocks gained strength on April 17, with key indices rising over 0.50%, supported by consumer goods and oil & gas sectors. The Nifty 50 and S&P BSE Sensex closed higher, while broader markets showed even stronger gains amid easing tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: Sensex gains 500 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,350; investors earn ₹5 lakh crore in a day
items.summary: The Sensex rose by 505 points, or 0.65%, to end at 78,493.54, while the NSE barometer Nifty 50 settled at 24,353.55, rising by 157 points, or 0.65%.

items.title: Banks' gross NPA's to stay range-bound at 2.0-2.2 per cent by March 2027 despite West Asia risks: Crisil
items.summary: Indian banks are likely to keep asset quality under control over the next two years, with gross non-performing assets (NPAs) projected at 2.0-2.2 per cent by March 2027, only a shade higher than the estimated historic low of 2.0 per cent as of March 2026, according to ratingas agency Crisil Ratings. The resilience will be led by strong corporate balance sheets, even as the MSME segment faces pressure from the ongoing West Asia conflict.

items.title: Expert view: SAMCO CEO Jimeet Modi doesn't expect a runway rally on D-Street, sees market consolidating through 2026
items.summary: Jimeet Modi, CEO of SAMCO Group, offers a cautious perspective on the Indian stock market, anticipating a period of consolidation rather than a runaway rally through 2026. He believes the Q4 earnings season may remain muted.

items.title: NBCC sells 14 lakh sq ft commercial space in South Delhi for Rs 5,779 cr via e-auction
items.summary: NBCC has successfully sold commercial space in South Delhi. The sale of approximately 14 lakh square feet at Bharat Business Park fetched Rs 5,779 crore. This significant transaction was conducted through an e-auction. NBCC will receive a one percent marketing fee. These funds will support the redevelopment of residential colonies in the area.

items.title: Why is Nifty 50 facing resistance at 24,400 level? Experts list out these triggers
items.summary: Despite the positive undertone, the Nifty 50 continues to face stiff resistance in the 24,400–24,500 zone. The index has repeatedly failed to sustain gains above this level, having touched 24,400 in the previous session before retreating.

items.title: PNGRB's new LPG pipeline expansion plan to attract investment of Rs 12,500 crores
items.summary: PNGRB has initiated bidding for nine LPG pipeline projects spanning around 2,500 km to reduce reliance on road transport and improve safety and efficiency in LPG distribution. As per its announcement, four key pipelines are currently in the bidding stage, with an estimated investment of Rs 12,500 crore. The move aims to connect refineries and import terminals with bottling plants, enabling seamless supply while minimising risks associated with tanker transport. PNGRB also highlighted that shifting to pipelines will cut greenhouse gas emissions, lower transit losses, generate employment, and su

items.title: West Asia crisis: India resilient for now, but second-order inflation pain is coming, warns Swaminathan Aiyar
items.summary: India might weather the West Asia conflict's economic storm if it ends soon, according to Swaminathan Aiyar. While a swift resolution is expected due to US political pressures, second-order effects like rising inflation and fertilizer costs are unavoidable. India's resilience is noted, but the possibility of a prolonged conflict poses significant risks.

items.title: Akshaya Tritiya 2026: How should you allocate gold and silver in your portfolio?
items.summary: As Akshaya Tritiya approaches, experts recommend a prudent allocation of 15% in bullion, favoring gold. While gold shows long-term potential, silver is suggested as a minor diversifier. A disciplined investment strategy is essential for managing market volatility.

items.title: Global Markets | China stocks slip as investors take profits and await possible US-Iran talks
items.summary: Chinese stocks dipped Friday as investors paused after recent gains, awaiting clarity on US-Iran talks. A ceasefire in Lebanon offered a glimmer of hope. While Shanghai and Hong Kong markets saw minor losses, analysts suggest Hong Kong could rebound faster. Energy security and manufacturing remain key themes for China, though export-oriented firms face potential Middle East instability risks.

items.summary: Aditya Birla Housing ceases to be a wholly-owned arm of Aditya Birla Capital

items.title: Israel's central bank chief pins hopes on peace as economic shock looms
items.summary: Israel's central bank governor hopes a swift resolution to the wars in Lebanon and Iran can ease a growth shock.

items.title: Wipro CEO Srini Pallia says market is highly competitive, highlights firm's robust pipeline, AI adoption
items.summary: Wipro's CEO Srini Pallia remains confident about the company's strong project pipeline. He indicates that ongoing global trade disruptions and tariff changes are not significantly impacting Wipro's business. While some specific client issues were noted in the American market, Pallia believes these will be managed. The company is focusing on leveraging AI for new projects and client solutions.

items.summary: Fintra processes Letters of Credit, Bank Guarantees, and Documentary Collections using specialised AI agents for document processing, compliance screening, clause recommendation and risk scoring

items.title: Reliance Jio IPO delayed? India's largest public offer has some good news in May
items.summary: Reliance Industries is reportedly planning to file draft IPO paperwork for Jio Platforms in May, incorporating full fiscal year earnings. This revised timeline, pushed back from March due to market downturns, aims to present a more favorable investor picture. The listing could be India's largest-ever IPO.

items.title: Citius Transnet InvIT IPO: Check GMP and subscription trends before you bid
items.summary: Citius Transnet InvIT's Rs 1,105 crore IPO opened with zero grey market premium, signaling a flat debut. The issue, focusing on road infrastructure, saw lukewarm subscription on day one, primarily from non-institutional investors. With a yield-focused structure, the listing is anticipated to hover near the issue price, offering limited immediate gains.

items.title: Over 30 MF schemes trimmed holdings in 8 stocks in March ’26. Do you own any?
items.summary: Mutual funds significantly reduced their exposure to equities in March 2026, with over 30 schemes cutting positions in eight specific stocks. These stocks collectively experienced substantial declines, with some losing as much as 33% in the first three and a half months of the year.

items.summary: State Bank ‌of ‌India, ⁠HDFC ⁠Bank, Bank of ​India among banks allowed to import gold, silver

items.title: Aluminium and copper to stay elevated, steel risky: CLSA's top metals and cement calls for FY27
items.summary: Base metals like aluminium and copper present significant opportunities, according to CLSA India's Indrajit Agarwal. Steel faces near-term downside risks. Aluminium prices are expected to rise due to supply disruptions and increasing demand. Cement sector anticipates earnings pressure now, with recovery expected later in the fiscal year. Indian producers have a cost advantage in non-ferrous metals.

items.title: Aditya Birla Housing Finance raises Rs 2,750 cr from Indriya via stake sale
items.summary: Aditya Birla Housing Finance has secured Rs 2,750 crore. This funding comes from selling a 14.29 percent stake to Indriya Ltd. Indriya Ltd is part of PE investor Advent International. Aditya Birla Housing Finance is no longer a wholly-owned subsidiary. Aditya Birla Capital now holds 85.505 percent of the housing finance firm.

items.title: Energy independence could make America more aggressive
items.summary: Being a net exporter means that energy shocks no longer constrain US foreign policy in the ways they once did

items.title: Indian refiners pay for Iran oil in yuan via ICICI Bank, sources say
items.summary: Indian refiners are making payments for Iranian oil using Chinese yuan. This is happening through ICICI Bank's Shanghai office. Indian Oil and Reliance have bought shipments under a temporary U.S. sanctions waiver. This payment method has not been previously reported. India has also used yuan for Russian oil. The U.S. waiver on Iranian oil is set to expire soon.

items.title: Has Nifty bottomed out? ICICI Securities dissects chart patterns, predicts these 21 stocks could outperform
items.summary: After an 18-month consolidation and a 16% correction, ICICI Securities says Nifty 50 may have formed a durable bottom, supported by historical patterns and strong technical levels. The brokerage expects potential upside ahead and highlights banks, energy names and select midcaps as likely outperformers in the next market upcycle.

items.title: Govt allows SBI, HDFC, Axis Bank & 12 others to import gold, silver until March 2029
items.summary: The central government ​has ​permitted 15 top banks, including ‌the ⁠State ⁠Bank ​of India, HDFC ​Bank, Bank ​of ⁠India and ‌others ​to ​import ⁠gold and silver from ​April 1, ​2026 to March ‌31, 2029.

items.title: India targets over ten-fold expansion in nuclear power capacity from 8.8 GW to 100 GW by 2047: CEA Chairperson
items.summary: India plans a significant expansion of its nuclear power capacity, targeting 100 gigawatts by 2047. This ambitious goal represents a more than tenfold increase from the current 8.8 gigawatts. The Central Electricity Authority is working on legislative changes and streamlining processes to achieve this.

items.title: Multibagger defence stock Apollo Micro Systems zooms 16% after this nod from the Government of India
items.summary: Multibagger defence stock Apollo Micro Systems skyrocketed 16% after it received a license for the manufacture of ammunition, which is valid for a lifetime. The stock has delivered multibagger returns to investors of over 3,000% in five years.

items.title: Small-cap stock under ₹50 hits upper circuit, rises over 470% in one year
items.summary: Take Solutions rose 2% after the firm entered India's longevity and anti-aging market, aiming to create a scalable platform for health products and digital solutions. The global longevity market is projected to grow significantly, with a focus on innovative and technology-driven health solutions.

items.title: Ahana announces RBI reporting solution for co-operative banks using its proprietary Data Model
items.summary: Ahana introduces a new solution for co-operative banks. It aims to simplify Reserve Bank of India reporting. The system uses a special data model. This will reduce manual work and errors. Banks can expect faster reporting cycles. Audit readiness will also improve. The solution consolidates data for better accuracy and consistency.

items.title: JSW One in talks for $350–400 million IPO, pre-IPO round underway
items.summary: JSW One Platform is in early talks with bankers for a $350–400 million IPO and is planning a ₹650–700 crore pre-IPO round to benchmark valuation ahead of a potential year-end listing.

items.title: Asia markets mostly fall as fragile Middle East ceasefire tempers sentiment
items.summary: Most Asia-Pacific markets traded lower Friday as an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire tempered risk appetite, even after Wall Street closed at fresh record highs.

items.title: Why did Apex Frozen Foods, Avanti Feeds shares zoom up to 15% today? Explained
items.summary: According to a report in Business Standard, India's marine exports rose 14% year-on-year in FY26, defying odds as the period was marked by high US tariffs of 50% and also the Middle East crisis at the end of the fiscal.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $74,600, Ethereum steady above $2,300 amid volatility; technicals signal strength
items.summary: Bitcoin trades near $75,000 while Ethereum remains steady above the $2,300 mark amid the market volatility.

items.title: India's first maritime-focused lender eyes $1 bln FY27 fundraise
items.summary: Sagarmala Finance Corp. will raise 100 billion rupees in FY27. This funding will support ports, shipbuilding, and waterways. The company will borrow through bonds, term loans, and foreign currency. Sagarmala aims to disburse up to 90 billion rupees in loans by March 2027. It also seeks government equity infusion to support its growth.

items.title: Spain touts energy resilience to Iran war as Trump tensions cast shadow over trade
items.summary: The southern European country has ramped up its investment in solar and wind technologies in recent years.

items.title: India smartphone shipments fall to 6-year low in Q1 amid price hikes: Counterpoint Research
items.summary: India's smartphone shipments experienced a 3% year-on-year decline in Q1 2026, reaching a six-year low due to cost pressures and price hikes. Analysts predict further declines, with Vivo leading the market share. Google emerged as the fastest-growing premium brand, boosted by AI features.

items.title: NTPC Green Energy group firm to begin 150 MW supply from solar project on Saturday
items.summary: On April 18, 2026, NGEL, under the NTPC umbrella, is set to launch its 150 MW solar power output from a pioneering project in Rajasthan. This endeavor, part of the 300 MW Project Sixteen Renewable Power Private Ltd., marks a significant milestone as NGEL's total capacity will hit 10,276.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty hold gains led by FMCG stocks rally, Wipro, HDFC Life top losers
items.summary: Broader markets outperformed the benchmarks, with the midcap index rising 0.8% and the smallcap index advancing over 1% at the time of writing

items.title: FIIs grow more wary of India as FX curbs hit bonds, earnings risks haunt equities
items.summary: Foreign investors face higher costs and complexity hedging against rupee swings due to India's forex rules. This, coupled with war-related earnings concerns, is impacting Indian bonds and equities. Overseas investors have sold Indian government debt and shares. Lingering currency stability worries and high oil prices are dampening sentiment.

items.title: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei slips from record high on tech drag
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei share index saw a dip on Friday, pulling back from its record peak. Investors took profits in technology stocks, especially chip-related companies, following rapid gains. This cautious move came after US markets reached new highs. Tokyo Electron and SoftBank Group were among the decliners. The broader Topix also experienced a fall.

items.title: Global Markets | South Korean shares dip, but mark second weekly gain on Iran ceasefire hopes
items.summary: South Korean stocks ended lower Friday but secured a second weekly gain. Hopes for a U.S.-Iran ceasefire boosted sentiment. The Korean won weakened against the dollar. Bond yields saw an increase. Foreign investors were net sellers of shares. The KOSPI index experienced a slight dip. This market movement reflects ongoing geopolitical and economic factors.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty hold steady at midday; HUL, Nestle lead gains as IT, Financials weigh
items.summary: The session’s tone has been shaped by easing geopolitical concerns, particularly around US-Iran diplomatic engagement, which has moderated crude oil prices and tempered near-term inflation worries

items.title: Kalyan Jewellers, Titan, and other jewellery stocks tumble up to 6% while MMTC rallies 16%. Here's why
items.summary: Indian banks have reportedly halted gold and silver import orders, causing jewellery stocks like Kalyan Jewellers and Titan to fall. MMTC, however, surged on market rumors of being permitted to import gold. This situation has led to significant amounts of precious metals stuck at customs, awaiting a formal government order, potentially impacting supply ahead of Akshaya Tritiya.

items.title: India's economy slipping to 6th rank, IMF data shows, prospects bright
items.summary: India has slipped to the sixth largest economy in 2025 in nominal terms, according to the International Monetary Fund, but it remains the fastest-growing major economy. The IMF projects India’s growth at 6.5% in 2026, far ahead of the United Kingdom.

items.title: Alt Capital launches AYF III, ties up with FundsIndia Private Wealth to raise Rs 1,000 crore
items.summary: Alt Capital has launched AltCap Yield Fund III to tap opportunities in commercial real estate, targeting Rs 1,000 crore with a green shoe option. The fund will focus on pre-leased Grade A assets, aiming for stable income and 16–18% IRR, backed by strong demand and the firm’s proven investment track record.

items.title: Nvidia rival tells CNBC it's seeking at least $100 million in funding as European AI chip market booms
items.summary: Investor interest for AI chip startups is rising, but big challenges remain for the nascent sector.

items.title: Finolex Cables looking to increase its North East presence driven by govt's infra, electrification push
items.summary: Finolex Cables is expanding its reach in Northeast India, focusing on Assam. Government investments in infrastructure and electrification are driving this growth. The company expects the Northeast to become a significant contributor to its business in the coming years.

items.title: Up 3150% in five years! Multibagger circuit-to-circuit stock hits upper circuit for third day in a row
items.summary: Elitecon International share price has fallen by over 18% in one month, and has declined 57% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis. The small-cap stock has dropped 72% in six months, while it has risen over 12% in one year.

items.summary: Kalyan Jewellers sets board meeting date to announce Q4 results 2026, final dividend for FY26

items.summary: Domestic gold prices were trading around ₹153,200 rupees per 10 gm on Friday

items.title: Wipro’s massive buyback fails to mask challenges caused by persistent growth problem
items.summary: Wipro's Q4 performance fell short due to macro headwinds and client issues, with a revenue growth of just 0.2%. Its Q1FY27 outlook is negative, suggesting continued challenges, while the company also announced a significant buyback and dividend for shareholders.

items.title: Wipro announces Rs 15,000-crore buyback: Check record date, price, other key things to know
items.summary: Wipro announced a Rs 15,000 crore share buyback at Rs 250 per share, its first in nearly three years. items.title: Maran claims SpiceJet owes over ₹400 cr with interest as payment dispute lingers
items.summary: Kalanithi Maran claims SpiceJet owes over ₹400 crore in arbitration, far exceeding the airline's stated ₹144.5 crore. The Delhi high court is reviewing SpiceJet's plea for relief from a deposit order.

items.title: West Asia War: India’s BBB rating seen stable despite energy shock; S&P flags corporate stress, weaker credit growth
items.summary: West Asia War: India's sovereign rating remains stable despite rising energy costs. However, supply disruptions for food and fuel could impact company credit quality. Top companies may see earnings drop, increasing debt ratios. Sectors like refining and airlines are most vulnerable. Banks might see slower credit growth. Government revenue could be affected by fuel price support measures.

items.title: Don't call time on dollar dominance just yet, say analysts as 'petroyuan' call sparks debate
items.summary: The dollar index, which tracks its performance against a basket of major currencies, fell almost 10% through 2025.

items.title: EXIM Bank says repayments unaffected by West Asia conflict
items.summary: EXIM Bank of India reports no impact on client repayments from the Middle East conflict. Indian companies in the region, primarily in construction, may see business affected. While current repayments are strong, prolonged conflict could pose challenges. Project awards and execution timelines might be impacted. However, reconstruction efforts could bring future opportunities. Rupee depreciation benefits EXIM Bank's balance sheet.

items.title: The costly voting structure for US funds needs an overhaul
items.summary: The current outdated system was designed for a very different era

items.title: EU's proposed CBAM expansion may impact Indian exporters: GTRI
items.summary: The European Union is set to broaden its Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism, which could lead to a spike in carbon tax expenses for Indian goods shipped to Europe. Exporters in India must quickly adapt by enhancing their emissions tracking and implementing decarbonisation strategies.

items.title: How El Nino-driven weaker monsoon rains could impact India
items.summary: The monsoon outlook for India in 2026 paints a worrying picture, with an anticipated El Niño likely to disrupt normal rainfall patterns. This scenario threatens to diminish crop production and necessitate greater imports of essential commodities, such as edible oils. Moreover, diminished rainfall could strain the power sector, necessitating governmental response.

items.title: ‘Guns vs. butter’: IMF flags tough trade-offs as governments ramp up defense spending
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund has raised the alarm over a global “guns versus butter” trade-off as countries ramp up defense spending.

items.title: EU proposes that Google allow third-party search engines access to data — What we know
items.summary: The European Commission proposed measures for Google to comply with the Digital Markets Act, by allowing third-party search engines access to search data. A final decision is expected in July this year.

items.title: Signature Global ties up with Tonino Lamborghini to develop ₹2,900 crore luxury housing project in Gurugram
items.summary: The branded residential venture, named ‘Tonino Lamborghini Residences Gurugram’, will occupy 12.40 acres in Sector 71 and feature 812 high-end apartments.

items.title: Vedanta accuses JAL creditors of preaching ‘process’ while ignoring it themselves
items.summary: The company is contesting the selection of Adani Group's lower-value bid in the NCLAT, citing a lack of transparency over payment structures and upfront cash.

items.title: UK economy grew faster than expected in February ahead of Iran war
items.summary: The economy saw its biggest monthly rise in more than two years just before the outbreak of the US-Israeli war with Iran.

items.title: Central government workers seek wages based on five-member families
items.summary: Central government employees are demanding a significant overhaul of their pay structure. They want the government to consider a family of five for pay calculations, moving away from the current three-member system. This includes support for dependent parents, aligning with legal mandates.

items.title: India's trade gap may widen in FY27 amid global and oil risks: Report
items.summary: India's trade deficit narrowed in March, but this improvement may not last. Experts warn of a widening deficit in the coming months. Factors like external shocks, slowing global demand, and import pressures are expected to impact trade. Exports face challenges due to weak global demand. The current account deficit is projected to widen.

items.title: Iran war sparks 'fundamental energy transition' in Seoul toward renewables: Energy minister
items.summary: Kim said South Korea will focus on wind and solar power to achieve its goal of reaching 100 gigawatts renewable energy capacity

items.summary: Tonino Lamborghini has partnered Signature Global for a 812-unit luxury development, as global brands tap rising demand for high-end residences

items.summary: Hong Kong is rolling out a new tax break for commodity traders as it seeks to strengthen its position as a regional trading hub and revive shipping activity amid global supply disruptions.

items.summary: The tribunal has asked the chipmaker to disclose withdrawal of India-specific warranty policy and outline how it will inform consumers.

items.summary: ‘The launch of direct flights to Hanoi strengthens our presence in South Asia and marks another step in our effort to build a network that connects India with dynamic global destinations’

items.summary: The government released trade data for March, the month severely impacted by the West Asia war. The data showed a sizeable decline in both exports and imports, especially with the top Gulf trade partners.

items.summary: Peyush Bansal has dismissed allegations that Lenkart allows employees to wear a hijab and turban, but restricts symbols such as a bindi, tilak or a kalawa. Details here.

items.summary: Uttar Pradesh has produced 89.26 lakh tons so far, against last year production of 91.10 lakh tons.

items.summary: The better-than-expected GDP data comes as Asian countries have been hit hard by the impact of the conflict.

items.summary: Bank of England governor says the Iran war energy shock makes the next interest rate decision "very, very difficult".

items.title: Tejas Networks flags ‘disappointing’ FY26 as BSNL slowdown, order delays hit revenue
items.summary: Completion of the 4G rollout and delayed orders pushed the Tata-backed firm into losses in FY26, with inventory overhang and a pending BSNL add-on weighing on recovery.

items.summary: Small towns are fueling India’s e‑commerce surge, with Amazon and Flipkart chasing new shoppers, faster delivery and rising aspirational demand.

items.title: CtrlS Datacenters weighs $300 million India IPO amid digital infrastructure boom
items.summary: The Hyderabad-based firm has begun talks with bankers, targeting a listing by the end of this fiscal year amid rising deal activity and capacity expansion in India’s digital infrastructure sector.

items.summary: An insurer reports a 71% rise in fraudulent claims, driven partly by an increase in faked images.

items.summary: The aid is roughly equivalent to a year's worth of crude oil imports by Asean countries.

items.summary: Brent and WTI edged lower as ceasefire hopes and fresh Iran-US talks countered supply fears from Hormuz disruptions and escalating tensions across West Asia.

items.summary: The company is selling off its shoe brand as it plans to shift to providing technology infrastructure.

items.title: Indian envoy to US meets US Trade Representative amid talks on finalising trade deal
items.summary: India’s Ambassador to the US Vinay Mohan Kwatra met top American officials to advance talks on a bilateral trade agreement, as both sides refine key terms. With tariffs and global trade rules shifting, a deal is expected once the US finalises its new tariff framework.

items.title: Former Tata Trustee Mehli Mistry wants an administrator to run Tata Trusts
items.summary: Mistry challenged the votes cast by Venu Srinivasan and Vijay Singh that led to his ouster in October; questioned Tata Trusts chair Noel Tata's objection to reappointing N. Chandrasekaran as Tata Sons chair; highlighted what he claims is a procedural flaw in Noel's appointment as the Trusts chair.

items.title: More than rooms: India’s hotels load up on restaurants, banquets and spas
items.summary: Hotel stays in India are becoming bundled experiences. A room is only one part of what is being paid for. As long as demand continues to favour these full-service formats, and hotels continue to build around them, room rates are unlikely to ease.

items.title: After a decade of bankruptcy law, top conglomerates Adani, JSW, Reliance, Tata emerge as biggest winners
items.summary: Together, these four conglomerates acquired companies that accounted for nearly a quarter of the total admitted claims of ₹13 trillion under the corporate insolvency resolution process of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code as of December 2025

items.summary: The startup that built its name on easy consumer credit is undergoing a radical makeover as a regulated bank, trading its risky roots for a very different kind of future.

items.title: India's trade gap shrinks in March, swells in FY26
items.summary: India's trade deficit saw a reduction in March as the West Asia conflict disrupted trade flows. Exports and imports both declined, narrowing the monthly deficit. However, the overall trade deficit for the fiscal year widened. Despite challenges, India achieved record exports of $860 billion in FY26, showcasing resilience. Several free trade agreements are expected to boost future trade prospects.

items.title: Back to books - Sweden's schools cutting back on digital learning
items.summary: Swedish classrooms swap laptops for books, pens and paper, raising concerns from the tech sector.

items.summary: Experts warn of hidden risk of counterfeits, while the government consults on stricter product safety rules.

items.summary: The lawsuit said the firm's practices had led to higher ticket prices and worse service for customers.

items.title: Struggling shoe retailer Allbirds makes bizarre pivot to AI, adds $127 million in value
items.summary: Allbirds announced a deal with American Exchange Group to sell its intellectual property and other assets for $39 million in March.

items.title: Govt's wheat procurement to drop 69% due to delayed arrivals
items.summary: Wheat procurement is set to drop significantly in the 2026-27 marketing year. Unseasonal rains delayed grain arrivals and affected quality. This has led to a sharp decline in current procurement figures compared to last year. The government has approved some wheat and wheat product exports after assessing domestic supply and prices.

items.title: Roblox gaming platform reaches $12 million settlement with Nevada enhancing youth protections
items.summary: Roblox gaming platform reaches $12 million settlement with Nevada enhancing youth protections

items.title: WPI inflation hits 38-month high of 3.9% in March as soaring energy, crude prices amid West Asia war drive up costs
items.summary: India's wholesale inflation reached a 38-month peak of 3.9% in March. This surge was fueled by rising costs of crude oil, energy, and manufactured goods. Experts anticipate further increases due to ongoing global tensions. Supply disruptions also contributed to higher input prices across various sectors. Retail inflation also saw a slight uptick.

items.title: US war on Iran was a 'mistake', says Reeves
items.summary: The chancellor's criticism follows a report that the conflict will hit the UK harder than other big economies.

items.title: India trade delegation to visit US for BTA talks, first in-person meeting since October
items.summary: An Indian trade team led by Darpan Jain will visit the US from April 20–22 for talks on a bilateral trade agreement, the first in-person meeting since October. Officials said the deal is being recalibrated amid changing tariff conditions, while India also rejected US allegations in a Section 301 probe.

items.title: Saudi Wealth Fund Sets New Strategy to Build Global Champions
items.summary: Saudi Arabia’s sovereign wealth fund will step up efforts to boost returns and build portfolio companies into global champions as the kingdom contends with the economic costs of the Iran war.

items.title: US’ 301 probes: India denies allegations, seeks termination of both investigations
items.summary: India has rejected allegations by the US Trade Representative under its Section 301 probe, denying claims of “structural excess capacity” in sectors such as petrochemicals and textiles. It argued that its $42 billion trade surplus with the US is a macroeconomic outcome shaped by multiple factors and not due to unfair practices.

items.title: India stands by trade pact with US, trade negotiators to visit Washington next week
items.summary: An Indian trade delegation will visit the US to discuss a bilateral trade agreement. Negotiations will focus on recalibrating the deal due to recent tariff changes. India aims to secure favorable terms and commitments. This engagement follows a call between Prime Minister Modi and President Trump. Discussions will also cover trade issues and investigations.

items.title: Electronics imports cross USD 100 billion in 2025-26: Commerce Ministry
items.summary: India's electronics imports surged past 100 billion US dollars in 2025-26. This marks a significant increase from the previous fiscal year. Despite a manufacturing push, the country remains reliant on imported components. Electronics exports also saw growth, driven by smartphone shipments, but imports continue to outpace them.

items.title: India achieves record exports of USD 860 billion in 2025-26: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India has set a new export record, reaching $860 billion in shipments for the fiscal year 2025-26. This achievement surpasses the previous year's $825 billion. The nation's exports demonstrate resilience against global challenges. Nine new trade agreements are expected to boost this growth further, opening new markets and opportunities across various sectors.

items.title: NBCFDC logs highest ever disbursement of Rs 613.75 crore in FY26: Govt
items.summary: The National Backward Classes Finance and Development Corporation recorded its highest-ever disbursement of Rs 613.75 crore in FY 2025-26. This benefited over 61,000 individuals, marking a significant increase from the previous year. The corporation also saw full utilization of funds under various schemes, including VISVAS and PM-DAKSH.

items.title: India-UK trade agreement may come into force next month: Commerce Secy
items.summary: India's trade deals are set to boost exports. The pact with the UK is expected in May. Oman's agreement is slated for June 1. New Zealand's FTA is anticipated on April 27. The India-EU FTA is targeted for this calendar year. These agreements will open new avenues for Indian businesses.

items.title: Shoemaker Allbirds stock jumps nearly 400% after rebranding to AI company
items.summary: Allbirds shares soared close to 400% after announcing a shift to AI compute infrastructure, backed by a $50 million financing deal. The company plans to rebrand as ‘NewBird AI’ and exit the footwear market.

items.title: LIC must bridge tech gap with private peers, says DFS secretary Nagaraju
items.summary: Nagaraju said he was ‘a little surprised’ to learn LIC was among the first companies in India to adopt mainframe systems, adding, ‘we must reflect on why we have fallen behind in recent years in technology adoption’.

items.title: India’s unemployment rate edges up to 5.1% in March as urban joblessness rises
items.summary: The unemployment rate in urban areas rose to 6.8% in March from 6.6% the previous month, while the rural unemployment rate remained nearly unchanged, said the latest Periodic Labour Force Survey Monthly Bulletin.

items.title: Tata Motors bets on flexible powertrain production lines even as EV bus momentum grows
items.summary: Tata Motors is avoiding dedicated EV bus production lines, opting for flexible manufacturing to respond to shifting demand across diesel, CNG and electric buses in a tender-driven market.

items.title: Snap to axe 1,000 staff: Snapchat parent faces pressure from activist investor, shares climb over 5% in premarket
items.summary: The Snapchat parent company will also eliminate over 300 vacant positions, aiming to slash annualized expenses by more than $500 million by the latter half of this year.
